title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues after its engine burn toward the moon. The mission remains on track as it cruises toward its lunar destination.

title: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
summary: Italy's Argotec has opened its first U.S. satellite production facility near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to meet increasing demand from American space programs. The company plans to scale this facility to support further growth.

title: White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts
summary: The White House has proposed significant budget cuts for NASA for the second year in a row, which would heavily affect the agency's science programs and the International Space Station.

title: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are in a dispute over Amazon's broadband satellite constellation deployment plans, with SpaceX alleging safety risks. Amazon denies the claims and plans to revise its deployment strategy.

title: Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’
summary: Europe's role in the 'second space age' hinges on achieving strategic autonomy in space, reducing reliance on foreign technology. This push for self-sufficiency is becoming increasingly critical for the European Union.

title: China’s commercial Tianlong-3 rocket fails on debut launch
summary: China's commercial Tianlong-3 rocket failed during its maiden launch due to an anomaly during ascent. The debut launch of the rocket, developed by Space Pioneer, ended in failure.

title: Artemis 2 heads to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has begun its journey to the moon following a successful maneuver on April 2. This marks a significant step toward the agency's lunar exploration goals.

title: Swift spacecraft reorientation buys time for reboost mission
summary: NASA adjusted the operations of the Swift spacecraft in a decaying orbit to extend its lifespan until a planned reboost mission later this year. This reorientation strategy aims to preserve the mission's scientific capabilities before the orbit-raising attempt.

title: Former Sierra Space CEO Tom Vice to lead Astrion
summary: Tom Vice, former CEO of Sierra Space, has been appointed to lead Astrion, a Huntsville-based defense contractor specializing in systems engineering and space mission assurance.

title: Optical terminals still a bottleneck in Pentagon’s proliferated constellation
summary: The Pentagon faces challenges with optical communications terminals for its proliferated constellation, with GP Sandhoo stating that the required quantity is not yet achievable. The bottleneck highlights delays in meeting the demand for advanced space communication technology.

title: It's official: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break humanity's all-time distance record
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break the all-time distance record from Earth, reaching 252,757 miles on April 6. This surpasses the previous record set by Apollo 13 in April 1970.

title: Astronauts on the ISS watched NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space
summary: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station (ISS) observed NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space. The event marked a significant moment shared between Earth and low Earth orbit.

title: 1st results from Blue Ghost lunar lander reveal how much we still don't know about the moon
summary: Initial data from Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost lunar lander challenges the traditional view of the moon's thermal division, suggesting more complexity than previously understood. The findings highlight significant gaps in our knowledge about lunar thermal characteristics.

title: Watch Atlas V rocket launch its heaviest-ever payload early on April 4
summary: An Atlas V rocket is set to launch 29 of Amazon's internet satellites on April 4, marking the heaviest payload it has ever carried. The launch will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts see Earth in the rear-view mirror | Space photo of the day for April 3, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a stunning photo of Earth as they traveled away from the planet toward the Moon. This milestone marks a significant moment in their lunar mission preparations.

title: NASA is sending astronauts back to the moon. Can you see the Artemis 4 landing sites from Earth?
summary: NASA's Artemis Program aims to return astronauts to the moon, specifically targeting the lunar south pole. The mission raises questions about the visibility of potential landing sites from Earth.

title: Pulsars to the extreme: Spinning dead stars found blasting radio signals from the 'edge of their magnetic reach'
summary: Astronomers discovered that rapidly spinning pulsars emit radio signals from the edges of their magnetic fields. This phenomenon occurs as these extreme dead stars push their magnetic reach to the limit.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are cruising to the moon. So why are they doing CPR tests today?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are preparing for their moon mission, including conducting CPR tests. Flight Day 3 of their mission is expected to be more relaxed.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply launch to the International Space Station. The Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying 11,000 pounds of cargo, will launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers
summary: NASA has appointed Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia as its first Artemis II science officers to integrate advanced lunar science into Mission Control. Their roles will guide future Artemis crews in observing and analyzing the Moon.

title: Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom
summary: The news commemorates the 100th anniversary of Virgil I. 'Gus' Grissom's birth, highlighting his role as one of NASA's first seven astronauts and his historic spaceflight aboard Liberty Bell 7 in 1961.

title: Hello, World
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman captured a stunning image of Earth from the Orion spacecraft during the Artemis II mission, showing two auroras and zodiacal light. This is the first such photo taken from the spacecraft after completing the translunar injection burn.

title: Barents Sea Tied to Low Arctic Sea Ice
summary: Patches of open water in the Barents Sea contributed to the Arctic's record-low sea ice extent in March 2026, matching the lowest maximum observed in satellite records.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully completed a translunar injection burn, sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in over 50 years. The six-minute engine firing propelled the Orion spacecraft out of Earth's orbit toward lunar trajectory.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission will launch four astronauts aboard the Space Launch System rocket and Orion spacecraft on a 10-day journey around the Moon in April 2026. The crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
summary: The February 2026 eruption at Piton de la Fournaise on Réunion Island has lasted longer and produced more lava than recent eruptions. The lava has now reached the sea, extending its impact.

title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
summary: NASA launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The SLS rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying four astronauts on a historic journey.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: In March 2026, strong Harmattan winds carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and toward the Canary Islands, significantly reducing visibility and triggering weather alerts.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration, building on the legacy of the Apollo program.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz from locations including Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory America is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The company's involvement highlights its growing importance in the space industry.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California for the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another successful rideshare mission for SpaceX.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch but faced an unexpected delay.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028. The initiative was revealed by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on ground infrastructure at Starbase in preparation for the next generation of Starship vehicles, Block 3. This effort highlights SpaceX's focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry developments. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, showcasing advancements in integrated circuit technology. This innovation could have significant implications for the space industry's computing and electronics capabilities.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. This marks a significant recovery for the mission team.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 Ministerial Meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The mission is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 software on March 30, 2026, requiring all suppliers to use it for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky and expensive solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the 2026 request while reducing allocations for science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper'. The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and charts defining the space industry in Q1 2026, with a focus on the resurgence of Earth Observation (EO) capabilities. It highlights the growth and advancements in this sector during the first quarter of 2026.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year, exciting CLPS companies. The initiative is seen as a major opportunity for these companies to participate in lunar missions.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with four astronauts, including the first woman and first person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The crew includes Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, emerged from stealth with $500,000 in funding to develop an uncrewed space station for hosting manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris secured $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as a strategic step towards future Mars missions. The remarks highlight growing enthusiasm for NASA's Artemis program and long-term lunar exploration goals.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space technology development. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS capabilities.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching Iridium NTN Direct, a standardized global narrow-band satellite IoT service, with CEO Matt Desch highlighting its significance. The company is also advancing alternative PNT (Positioning, Navigation, and Timing) solutions.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has won a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for Europe under the ESA's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass has historically posed challenges for satellite services, but demand is growing as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is in discussions to potentially acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The deal, if finalized, could enhance Amazon's satellite and connectivity services.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. This funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force's innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate to develop a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing to safely remove satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies are urging the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency (SDA) acknowledges the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canadian telco Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed constellation for broadband services in Canada's North. The agreement will help improve connectivity in remote regions like Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation has acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation development. The acquisition aligns with Phantom Space's vertical integration strategy.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation have signed an MoU to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to enhance satellite-based IoT services.
